metadata.summary: TikTok account setup, profile optimization, content pillars for B2B/SaaS, video formats, and a phased ramp plan from launch to month 3+.

content.title: TikTok Basics & Setup
content.summary: TikTok account setup and content strategy for B2B/SaaS. Hook quality in the first 1-3 seconds is the highest-leverage variable. Creator accounts at launch, 3-4 content pillars, daily posting for 4 weeks, then scale to 3-5 videos/day.

why.p: TikTok delivers the highest organic reach of any social platform (1.5B+ MAU). Businesses that post corporate-style content fail; those who match the platform's native style — educational, authentic, trend-aware — can acquire customers at near-zero CAC. The HOOK→RETAIN→CONVERT→COMPOUND pattern is how the For You Page algorithm works: watch time and completion rate drive distribution.

list.item: Launching a TikTok presence from zero for a B2B/SaaS or solo product.
Writing video script outlines (educational, build-in-public, demo formats).
Drafting bio copy and profile optimization for a new account.
Creating a 30-day content calendar across 3-4 defined pillars.
Analyzing competitor TikTok accounts to extract content patterns before launch.
Writing caption variants with different hooks for A/B testing.
Filming, editing, or uploading video — agents cannot operate mobile apps or video software.
Deciding whether TikTok is the right channel without audience data.
Generating trend-based content — trends expire in hours; training data is too stale.
Accounts targeting audiences in countries with TikTok restrictions.
Any automated posting workflow — violates TikTok ToS.

section.name: setup-and-strategy
section.p: Profile optimization, account type selection, content pillars for B2B/SaaS, and video format choices.

rule.statement: Use a Creator account at launch; switch to Business only after establishing consistent posting.
rule.rationale: Creator accounts receive better algorithmic distribution. Business accounts add analytics but reduce organic reach in early phases.

rule.statement: Bio must contain: what you do + who it is for + one CTA word, under 80 characters.
rule.rationale: Viewers decide in 2 seconds whether to follow; a vague bio converts at 1/3 the rate of a specific one.

rule.statement: Display name must include a searchable keyword (e.g., "SaaS Tips | Mike", not just "Mike").
rule.rationale: TikTok search indexes display names; keyword inclusion drives discoverability.

rule.statement: Define 3-4 content pillars before posting. Each pillar must map to an ICP pain point.
rule.rationale: Random content confuses the algorithm's audience-matching; consistent pillars signal which For You Pages to distribute on.

list.item: Educational: quick tips, how-tos (e.g., "3 VS Code shortcuts you need").
Behind the scenes: build in public (e.g., "Day 47 of building my startup").
Trends with a niche twist: use trending sound + product demo.
Storytelling: founder journey (e.g., "How I got my first 100 users").
Myth busting: challenge assumptions (e.g., "Why your landing page sucks").
Quick tips (15-30 sec): Educational content, highest completion rate.
Story time (60-90 sec): Journey and lessons learned.
Tutorial (60-180 sec): How-to content with demos.
POV/Skit (15-30 sec): Relatable humor for B2B founders.
Before/After (30-60 sec): Transformations and product results.

antipattern.description: Slow hooks — no pattern interrupt in the first 1 second.
antipattern.reason: Viewers scroll past; watch time drops below algorithm threshold for distribution.

antipattern.description: Corporate style content — polished, formal, product-feature-focused.
antipattern.reason: Does not fit TikTok's native casual style; algorithm detects low engagement and stops distributing.

antipattern.description: Overproduced video with professional studio quality.
antipattern.reason: Feels inauthentic; native smartphone quality outperforms studio quality on this platform.

antipattern.description: No CTA at the end of every video.
antipattern.reason: Viewers who finish without a follow prompt do not convert to followers or leads.

section.name: scripts-and-checklist
section.p: Video script structures for the three primary formats and a phased launch checklist from week 1 to month 3+.

rule.statement: Write the hook (0-3 seconds) before the rest of the script; if the hook is weak, discard the script.
rule.rationale: Hook quality is the single highest-leverage variable; a great body with a weak hook produces zero views.

rule.statement: Always script on-screen text alongside spoken text — they may differ for emphasis.
rule.rationale: Approximately 70% of TikTok is watched without sound; captions are not optional.

rule.statement: Constrain hook to 8-12 spoken words; constrain each body point to 15-25 words.
rule.rationale: Generated and improvised scripts run too long; explicit word-count constraints prevent watch-time collapse.

rule.statement: Post 1 video every day for the first 4 weeks before increasing frequency.
rule.rationale: Daily posting builds algorithm familiarity. Skipping days in the first 30 days resets distribution testing.

list.item: Week 1: Create account, research 10 niche accounts, define 3-4 pillars, plan first 7 videos.
Weeks 2-4: Post 1 video/day, test different hooks, track performance, engage comments within 1 hour.
Month 2: Increase to 2-3 videos/day, double down on top-performing pillar, start incorporating trends.
Month 3+: Aim for 3-5 videos/day, repurpose top content, test TikTok ads on best organic videos.

rule.statement: Provide the agent with 3-5 real high-performing videos in your niche as format references before requesting scripts.
rule.rationale: Without format references, agents produce structurally correct but tonally generic scripts that underperform platform-native content.

example.title: Prompt pattern for batch script generation
example.code:
You are writing TikTok video scripts for [PRODUCT] targeting [ICP]. Content pillars: [PILLAR1], [PILLAR2], [PILLAR3]. Format for each script: - Hook (0-3 sec, spoken + on-screen text identical) - Body (4-45 sec, 3 numbered points with brief demo note) - CTA (final 5 sec)
Generate 7 scripts for Pillar: [EDUCATIONAL TIPS]. Each hook must be different formula type (curiosity/challenge/promise/relatability/controversy). Output as JSON array: [{"hook": "...", "body": [...], "cta": "..."}]

section.name: tools-and-services
section.p: Tools for competitive research, analytics, editing, and scheduling on TikTok.

list.item: TikTok API (unofficial): Read public TikTok data (use with caution — against ToS). Various Python wrappers on PyPI.
yt-dlp: Download TikTok videos for competitive research. Install: pip install yt-dlp / github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp
FFmpeg: Trim clips, add watermark, convert formats for upload. Install: brew install ffmpeg / ffmpeg.org
Whisper: Transcribe competitor videos for content analysis. Install: pip install openai-whisper
TikTok Creative Center: SaaS (free). Trend and hashtag data; manual research only.
Pentos: SaaS. Partial — data export. TikTok analytics tracking; no posting API.
Tokboard: SaaS. No API. Viral video tracker; manual only.
Later: SaaS. REST API. Scheduling TikTok posts; requires manual video upload first.
Hootsuite: SaaS. REST API. Cross-platform scheduling.
CapCut: SaaS/mobile. No API. Best free editor for TikTok-native formats.
Opus Clip: SaaS. No public API. AI clip extraction from long-form; manual trigger.
Vidyo.ai: SaaS. No public API. Similar to Opus Clip.

section.name: agentic-workflows
section.p: How to use AI agents effectively in TikTok content creation.

The most useful agentic application is batch script generation: give the agent the 3-4 content pillars, the ICP, and the product description, then request a 4-week content calendar with one-paragraph script outlines per video. A second pass can expand selected outlines into full word-for-word scripts with hook/body/CTA structure. The human films and edits; the agent handles the ideation and scripting layer. Captioning and hashtag research can also be delegated to the agent given a completed video title and topic.

section.name: agent-limitations
section.p: Where AI agents struggle with TikTok workflows.

list.item: TikTok's official API is restricted to approved business partners; consumer posting automation is not available.
Hook effectiveness can only be validated through actual platform performance data, not predicted by agents.
Algorithm optimization requires A/B testing real videos; agents cannot simulate the For You Page.
Trend identification requires real-time data; agent knowledge has a cutoff date.
Content that works for one niche (e.g., SaaS founders) may completely fail for another (e.g., B2C lifestyle).
